rulings.Case Title: TOM O'CONNOR ET AL VS. LAUS INVESTMENT GROUP ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS LAUS INVESTMENT GROUP, SOPHIE LAU, JEFFREY LAU and GINA LAU'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all causes of action. All four of defendants' arguments made in support of their demurrer lack merit. There is nothing legally or factually problematic about using the defined term "Lau Defendants" to refer to alleged owners of the buildings. The individuals encompassed within that defined term are adequately alleged to be the owners because the entity defendant may not be legally distinct from the individuals and, in that event, the individuals are legally responsible for all contract and contract-like claims. The fraudulent concealment claim is pled with sufficient particularity. If defendants believe they need further detail, they may seek it via discovery. Plaintiffs adequately alleged extreme and outrageous conduct. Because all defendants filed an answer to the first amended complaint on May 11, 2021, no further response to the first amended complaint is needed. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PAUL CRUG VS. ROGER SEVERIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Roger Severin And Doggett-Severin Trusts Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANTS ROGER SEVERIN and DOGGETT-SEVERIN TRUST'S Notice Of Motion To Strike Claims For Punitive, Exemplary And Treble Damages From Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Mr. Crug alleged sufficient ultimate facts to show that defendants acted with malice and oppression as those terms are defined in Civil Code 3294(c) and to show entitlement to treble damages per the Rent Ordinance. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: PAUL CRUG VS. ROGER SEVERIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANTS ROGER SEVERIN and DOGGETT-SEVERIN TRUST'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to the Eleventh Cause of Action and SUSTAINED with Ten Calendar Days Leave to Amend as to the Twelfth Cause of Action. Defendants' reliance on Stoiber v. Honeychuck (1980) 101 Cal. App. 3d 903, 928 for the proposition that a tenant no longer in possession may not obtain restitution under the UCL lacks merit. In Stoiber the tenant did not seek restitution per the UCL and thus the decision had no occasion to decide whether a tenant not in possession may obtain restitution. ABC International Traders, Inc. v. Matsushita Electric Corp. (1997) 14 Cal 4th 1247, 1269-71 held that a party may obtain restitution per the UCL even if the party did not seek or obtain an injunction and there is no reason to believe that the ABC is inapplicable to a UCL based on unlawful conduct by a landlord. Defendants' argument based on the Health & Safety Code, even if correct, does not support their demurrer since the UCL claim is predicated on alleged unlawful conduct other than that proscribed by the Health & Safety Code. A demurrer lies only to the entirety of a claim. Rather than being supportive of Mr. Crug's assertion that he adequately alleged his constructive eviction claim, Stoiber, 101 Cal. App. 3d at 925-26 shows that the claim is insufficiently alleged. Stoiber says "Abandonment of the premises by the tenant within a reasonable time after the wrongful act of the landlord is essential to enable the tenant to claim a constructive eviction." There are no facts alleged to support this "essential" attribute of constructive eviction. Mr. Crug is granted leave, if he is able to do so, to allege ultimate facts showing that he abandoned the premises within a reasonable time after the condition(s) that made the premises unfit for occupancy. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VISTA DEL MONTE HOUSING LP VS. RICK RANSES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 7, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT RICK RANSES' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. The motion was filed prematurely prior to service of the summons and has been mooted by the filing of a proof of service by a registered process server attesting to proper substitute service on June 26, 2021. Defendant's invitation for this Court to decline to exercise jurisdiction based on asserted applicability of the CDC moratorium is not supported by any authority or facts appropriate for consideration on a motion to quash. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
